vulnerabilities.description: Webhook secret defaults to empty string if STAGEHOLDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var is not set. This disables HMAC signature verification for Hub webhooks, allowing attackers to forge webhook events (user updates, subscription changes, etc.). The fallback `?? ""` should be removed and the env var should be validated as required in env.validation.ts

STAGEHOLDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET is not included in the Zod environment validation schema, so missing webhook secrets won't be caught at application startup. Add validation: `STAGEHOLDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET: z.string().min(1, "...")` to catch misconfiguration early

Plaintext mode fallback when ENCRYPTION_KEY is not set. While properly logged as a warning and mitigated by env validation requiring the key in production, sensitive user data (journals, habits, todos) is stored unencrypted. Ensure ENCRYPTION_KEY is always set in production via deployment verification

recommendations: CRITICAL: Remove the `?? ""` fallback in app.module.ts line 66. Replace with `secret: process.env.STAGEHOLDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET` and add STAGEHOLDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET to env.validation.ts as a required string field with minimum length validation
Add STAGEHOLDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET to env.validation.ts with production-required validation (similar to IDENTITY_CLIENT_SECRET)
Verify in CI/CD that STAGEHOLDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET is populated in production deployments and log verification on startup
No SQL injection risks found — all database queries use Mongoose parameterized methods safely
No XSS vulnerabilities detected — no dangerouslySetInnerHTML or innerHTML usage; React auto-escaping in place
No hardcoded secrets — all sensitive values properly loaded from environment variables
Authentication/Authorization is properly enforced globally via StageholderAuthGuard as APP_GUARD; only legitimate public endpoints (health, webhook) are marked @Public
Encryption implementation uses industry-standard AES-256-GCM from @boringnode/encryption with proper key rotation scripts provided
HTTP exception filter properly suppresses sensitive error details from responses
Throttling is enabled (100 req/min) as a basic DoS protection
